channel.title: Posts on malekoa.com
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on malekoa.com

item.title: Dynamically Resizing Queue
item.description: This post builds on the Event Queue Service post I made earlier. In that post, I made an event queue service that could handle up to a fixed number of events. In this post, I will upgrade my queue implementation to dynamically resize itself when it reaches its capacity. Introduction In the previous post, I made an event queue service using a ring buffer. The ring buffer had a fixed size, and when it reached its capacity, it would not accept any more events.

item.title: Building an Event Queue Service Using Go
item.description: This post documents the process of developing an event queue service in Go. To see the full code, check out the Github repository Introduction When working with microservices, it’s important to keep your services decoupled. This makes it easier to change one component without affecting the others. But your services still need to communicate with each other. One way to do this is to have components send messages directly to each other.
